planner.metaDescription: Manage your team's resources with Taimbox's visual planner
planner.budgetOverrideTooltipMore: This month the project cap is {{hours}} h above the project budget.
planner.budgetOverrideTooltipLess: This month the project cap is {{hours}} h below the project budget.

dashboard.metaDescription: See in one place how workload, projects and profitability are progressing across your agency.

suspended.title: This account has been suspended
suspended.body: Contact support for more information. If you think this is a mistake, get in touch with us.

accountInactive.title: Your user is deactivated for this agency
accountInactive.body: You cannot access the app. Ask an admin to reactivate your profile if you need access again. Your historical data still counts in team reports and deadlines.

forgotPassword.description: Enter your email and we'll send you a link to reset your password.

success.title: Check your inbox
success.body: If the email is registered, you'll receive a link to reset your password.

toast.success: If the email exists in our system, you'll receive a recovery link.

register.footer: When you sign up, you'll create a new company and become its admin.

errors.nameTooShort: Name must be at least 2 characters long
errors.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 6 characters long
errors.agencyAlreadyExists: This company already exists. Please choose another name.

toast.loginAfterRegisterError: Account created but there was an error signing you in. Please sign in manually.

google.loadingSubtitle: Please do not close this window.
google.denied: Could not connect to Google Ads. The user denied access.
google.missingAgency: Could not identify the agency. Please try again from Integrations.

meta.loadingSubtitle: Please do not close this window.
meta.denied: Could not connect to Meta. Access denied.
meta.missingAgency: Could not identify the agency. Please try again from Integrations.
meta.dnsError: The integrations server could not resolve network/DNS (503). Check DNS for the edge-functions container or try again in a few seconds.

contact.title: Support
contact.subtitle: Send requests and track the status of your tickets for agency {{agency}}.
contact.noAgencySelected: Select an agency to contact support.

form.title: New request
form.description: Describe your question or issue. The support team will reply here.
form.messagePlaceholder: Describe your question or issue with as much detail as possible…
form.successBody: You can see the status and replies in "My tickets".

tickets.title: My tickets
tickets.description: List of your requests. Click "View" to see details and reply.

detail.title: Ticket detail
detail.conversationEmpty: There are no replies yet. The support team will reply here.
detail.loadError: The ticket could not be loaded.

toasts.agencyRequired: Select an agency to send the request.
toasts.createSuccess: Request sent. We'll reply as soon as possible.
toasts.createError: Error sending the request. Please try again.

projectList.monthRangeHint: The min/max shown apply to this month (deliverable proration or saved adjustment), not always the project budget before monthly proration or overrides.

global.notOwner: You can only delete your own assignments

month.noDeadlinesToDelete: There are no deadlines to delete for this month

previousMonth.noDeadlines: There are no deadlines in the previous month

toasts.noConfigToDelete: There is no saved configuration to delete

times.title: Time Tracking
times.disabledMessage: The time tracking module is not active in your agency. Enable it in Agency Settings to see who is tracking time.
times.subtitleActive: Who is tracking time right now and total for the day.

emptyState.title: No active timers
emptyState.description: When someone clicks Play on a planner task, it will appear here.

weeklyForecast.title: Monthly forecast
weeklyForecast.subtitle: Task change history and workload redistribution across teammates
weeklyForecast.redistributeCardDesc: Pick a project and move remaining hours to a teammate
weeklyForecast.noDelayedTasks: No overdue tasks in this project.

criticalLoad.title: Critical Load
criticalLoad.subtitle: Members with little to no availability

heatmap.title: Weekly Heatmap
heatmap.description: Quick view of who is overloaded or available.

blockedTasks.title: Tasks blocked by dependencies
blockedTasks.subtitle: Tasks that cannot progress until their dependency is completed

financialHealth.title: Financial Health
financialHealth.subtitle: Effective hourly rate, net margin and profitability per project and employee.

controls.starterPlan: Starter Plan: current and previous month

filters.operationalHelp: Isolates project cost from vacations and downtimes.
filters.dynamicHelp: Distributes payroll among actual month hours (total matches payroll).

columns.payrollImputedHelp: Payroll allocated to the project (operational or dynamic cost mode).
columns.commonOverheadHelp: Monthly common costs allocated to this project per your distribution rules.
columns.costTotalHelp: Allocated payroll plus allocated common costs (employee rows also include unattributed and operational idle where applicable).
columns.departmentTableHelp: Month revenue (accrued if current month), costs using the same rules as the project table, and margin.

costTooltip.commonNote: Includes monthly common costs allocated by hours. In operational mode payroll uses theoretical capacity; common costs use month hours (actual or computed per filter).
costTooltip.fallbackIntro: Dynamic calculation pending: not enough hours logged yet this month for exact payroll proration. Operational cost is shown temporarily.
costTooltip.fallbackCommon: Common costs keep being allocated from logged month hours in the selected mode (actual or computed).
costTooltip.standard: Sum of hours × operational hourly cost (theoretical monthly capacity).
costTooltip.dynamic: Sum of hours × dynamically prorated hourly cost. Payroll totals match payroll.

financialHealth.ehrTargetLoadedNote: The default target includes payroll and common costs configured for this month (loaded cost), unless you set a manual target here (Target & costs).

settings.dialogDescription: Set your effective hourly target and the costs allocated on this page (fixed per month or one-off).
settings.ehrNote: Minimum 1 €/h. If you do not save an explicit value, loaded cost or €75/h applies per page logic.

exportDiagnostic.tooltip: Download JSON for the visible month: active filters, projects, and per-employee profitability (names and amounts). For support or review; contains sensitive data—do not publish.

deliverablesLifecycle.heading: Deliverables — lifecycle
deliverablesLifecycle.subtitle: Full lifecycle view. No month selector.

commonExpenses.title: Monthly common costs
commonExpenses.description: Allocated to project and employee costs per your rules (global, one department, or percentage split).
commonExpenses.inViewRows: Allocated to rows in this view
commonExpenses.diffNote: With search filters or hidden projects, row allocation may be lower than the agency total.
commonExpenses.allocError: Could not compute common costs. Check percentages and amounts under Target & costs.
commonExpenses.zeroHourPeers: Some employees have no hours this month and receive no common-cost allocation: {{names}}

costHour.tooltipLoadedStd: Payroll operational base: {{baseH}} h/month theoretical. Payroll/h: {{p}} €/h; common costs/h: {{o}} €/h; loaded cost/h: {{c}} €/h.
costHour.tooltipLoadedDyn: Month base: {{baseH}} h. Payroll/h: {{p}} €/h; common costs/h: {{o}} €/h; loaded cost/h: {{c}} €/h.

costHelp.commonCosts: Common costs (Target & costs on this page) are added to hourly costs. With operational payroll, salary uses theoretical capacity; common costs follow logged hours in the selected mode.

employee.payrollInCostTooltip: Payroll charged to projects (rate × hours), plus unattributed hours and, in Operational mode, the slice of salary not covered by month hours (theoretical capacity > hours worked). Should match monthly payroll except rounding.
employee.standardIdleTooltip: In Operational mode the rate is payroll ÷ theoretical capacity. If you log fewer hours than that capacity, the gap to full monthly payroll appears here—you still pay the full salary.
employee.standardIdleRowTableTooltip: Remaining monthly payroll not explained by month hours × operational cost/h. No attributed revenue; reduces margin like a fixed monthly cost.
employee.expandMarginPerHourTooltip: This line’s margin (attributed revenue minus allocated payroll and common overhead) divided by hours. With proportional fee allocation, attributed revenue per hour always equals the project EHR; margin/h shows what remains after this person’s loaded cost.
employee.projectExpandCostPerHourTooltip: Loaded cost for this person on the project (payroll + allocated common overhead) divided by their hours. If this exceeds the project EHR, that time mix is below the contract’s revenue rate.
employee.expandProjectEhrTooltip: Project monthly fee divided by total project hours this month (same actual/computed mode). With proportional hours split, this is everyone’s attributed revenue rate; compare to loaded cost per hour via the Margin/h column.
employee.unattributedTooltipStd: Hours on internal tasks or outside this view. In Operational mode cost is computed against theoretical capacity, so the total may not exactly match payroll.
employee.unattributedTooltipDyn: Hours on internal tasks or projects outside this view. Added to cost so totals match monthly payroll.

project.costBreakdownTooltip: Made of attributed payroll + common costs. Hover the value to see the breakdown.

clientsAndProjects.title: Clients and Projects
clientsAndProjects.subtitle: Management of clients, budgets, and project tracking.

quick.label: Filters:
quick.noActivityTooltip: Projects without planned tasks this month
quick.needsPlanningTooltip: Projects that haven't planned all assigned or minimum hours
quick.needsPlanningDetail: If it has assigned hours, all must be planned. If it only has minimum, at least those must be planned.
quick.overBudgetTooltip: Projects with more planned hours than assigned

clientList.noResults: No clients found with these filters

agencies.title: Agencies
agencies.description: Manage your agencies or create a new one.

pacing.recommendedDailySub: to close the month on budget
pacing.googleNote: Taimbox calculates the monthly budget by summing the daily limits (x30.4) of your active campaigns in Google Ads. You can override this budget manually here to compare against a different target.
pacing.metaNote: The spending limit in Meta Ads is configured in Facebook (campaign, set or shared budget). Here you define the monthly target budget in Taimbox to compare real pace vs. target.
pacing.paceWarningGoogle: Spending pace is above the target to not exceed the monthly budget. Review campaigns in Google Ads, this usually happens if daily budgets sum more than the monthly target divided by 30.
pacing.paceWarningMeta: Spending pace is above the target to not exceed the monthly budget. Review and adjust limits in Meta Ads according to your structure (campaign, set or ad account).
pacing.forecastTooltip: Uses the average daily spend of the month (spent ÷ days elapsed) projected to the full calendar. If you recently changed the pace, it may not match the average daily target; that value is the pace to close within Taimbox's target budget.

configAccount.title: Configure account
configAccount.groupNote: Accounts from the same group are consolidated.
configAccount.hideAccountNote: Will not appear in the list

splitAccounts.title: Split Accounts
splitAccounts.description: Separate campaigns by keyword into virtual accounts.

newClient.title: New client
newClient.description: Create a new client to organize your projects.
newClient.name: Name

deleteClient.title: Delete client?
deleteClient.description: This action will delete the client and all associated projects. This action cannot be undone.

newProject.deliverableBlockIntro: Total contract amount and phase dates. The system prorates it across months to display the matching revenue in Profitability and progress in Operations Radar.

team.title: Team
team.subtitle: Manage employees, access, and schedules.
team.eventsDescription: Manage holidays, events, and other activities affecting the whole team.

limits.starterLimit: Your agency exceeds the Starter Plan limit. Upgrade to Pro or Business to add more employees.
limits.planReached: You have reached your plan's employee limit. Update your plan in Plan & Billing.

dashboard.internalDescription: Meetings, training, deadlines or other tasks.

common.name: Name
common.discardDescription: You have unsaved tasks. If you close now, you will lose the data entered.

agency.title: Agency Settings
agency.description: Manage the name, roles, departments, modules, and branding of your agency.

support.title: Contact Support
support.description: Send a help request or report a problem. Our support team will respond to you.

adAccounts.title: Advertising Account Management
adAccounts.description: Add your clients' account IDs here for the system to sync them.
adAccounts.label: Meta Ads Account ID
adAccounts.metaNote: For Meta Ads: The ID must include the prefix (e.g.: act_147...).
adAccounts.googleNote: Google Ads accounts are added automatically when syncing from the API.
adAccounts.saveSuccess: Meta Ads account added. Sync now to see data.

systemStatus.title: System Status
systemStatus.ok: System operational and connected to database.

agency.title: Agency Settings
agency.subtitle: Choose a section to view and edit settings

organization.cardDescription: Define your team areas (e.g. Marketing, Development). The color is used in the banner when filtering by department.

analytics.planningPrecisionDesc: Exclude projects or clients from the reliability index (planning precision). Useful for internal work you do not want weighted.
analytics.radarDesc: If the project name contains any of these words, it will not be flagged as “Low progress” risk at month end. E.g. pilot, maintenance.

general.title: General Information
general.name: Agency Name
general.profitabilityDesc: Effective hourly price target used on the Profitability page for KPIs and alerts
general.ehrNote: Positive values only; minimum 1 €/h. Use comma or dot as decimal separator (max 2 decimals). Default 75 €/h.
general.trackingDesc: Choose whether progress and profitability use client/budget hours (computed) or only time actually spent.
general.computedDesc: For completed tasks, reports and progress mainly use computed hours. They can differ from real effort (e.g. 1.5 h worked, 2 h billed for complexity or margin).
general.actualDesc: For completed tasks, reports and progress use actual hours; in the UI computed stays aligned with actual. No timer required—time can be entered manually.
general.dataExportDesc: Download JSON per month (deadlines, planning, coherence, radar, profitability, absences) for analysis or external reporting.

commonExpenses.title: Common costs (Profitability)
commonExpenses.description: Fixed monthly costs (from a start month) plus one-off lines per month. Allocated on Profitability by allocation type.
commonExpenses.recurringDescription: Same amount every month from the start date (and until the end date, if set). These add to the month-only lines you choose below.
commonExpenses.emptyRecurring: No fixed costs yet. Add rent, software, or other recurring items.
commonExpenses.monthOnlySectionDesc: Extra lines that count only in the selected month (history or one-offs).
commonExpenses.errRecurringFrom: Each fixed cost needs a valid start month (YYYY-MM).
commonExpenses.errRecurringUntil: If you set an end month, use YYYY-MM format.
commonExpenses.errRecurringRange: End month must be the same as or after the start month.
commonExpenses.copyEmpty: No data in the previous month to copy.
commonExpenses.emptyMonth: No lines for this month. Add costs or copy from the previous month.
commonExpenses.label: Label

distribution.label: Split method
distribution.helpHours: Proportional to hours logged this month. Employees with 0 h get no share.
distribution.helpHeadcount: Same share for every employee in scope, including those who don't use Taimbox.
distribution.helpPayroll: Proportional to monthly payroll: higher-paid employees absorb more overhead.

commonExpenses.copyConfirmDesc: This month already has lines. Copying from the previous month will replace them.
commonExpenses.errNegativeAmount: Each amount must be greater than or equal to 0.
commonExpenses.errDeptRequired: Pick a department for every “One department” line.
commonExpenses.errDeptUnknown: An invalid department was found. Choose one from the list.
commonExpenses.errSplitDept: Fill the department on every percentage row.
commonExpenses.errSplitSum: Percentage rows for one line must sum between 99.5 and 100.5.

modules.title: Active features
modules.description: Turn product areas in the menu on or off. Third-party connections are under Connections.
modules.seoReservedNote: The legacy agency “SEO” toggle is not wired to any screen in this version; the value is kept in data for compatibility.
modules.seoDesc: Audits, rankings and organic positioning KPIs.
modules.ppcEffect: Affects: Google Ads / Meta Ads menu entries and /ads, /meta-ads routes (plus role permissions).
modules.weeklyFeedbackDesc: Weekly forecast and planner close flow; with the Weekly integration enabled under Connections, closed weeks can’t be tweaked manually on the calendar.
modules.professionalGoalsDesc: Per-employee goals in the Objectives view.
modules.professionalGoalsEffect: Affects: Objectives menu entry and /okrs route (plus role permission).
modules.deadlinesDesc: Monthly hour targets per project and person, Deadlines page, and coherence with planning.
modules.deadlinesEffect: Affects: Deadlines menu entry and /deadlines route (plus role permission).
modules.timeTrackerEffect: Affects: Times menu entry and /tiempos route (plus role permission and plan).
modules.maxHoursDesc: Safety limit to avoid errors when stopping the tracker.

notifications.eventsDescription: Hybrid default: on so teams don’t miss when a blocking task is completed. Turn it off here for the whole agency if you prefer no email.
notifications.title: Email notifications
notifications.description: Set per-agency rules (transfers and scheduled reviews). The floating “Save changes” button also saves these rules.
notifications.dependencyUnblockHint: Sends a personalized email to each assignee of a task that depended on the completed one (if they have an email on their profile). The person who completed the blocking task is not notified.

rules.sectionHint: With several rules, expand each panel to edit without scrolling a long page.
rules.intro: Choose when and who receives email alerts (same channel as invitations or password reset). Scheduled rules are checked automatically based on frequency and time. If you self-host Taimbox, whoever runs the stack should enable the periodic background check; on the managed service you don’t configure anything extra here.
rules.empty: No rules yet. Add one to send email alerts (e.g. when a task transfer is requested).
rules.cardFootnote: Sending is secure: you only set criteria and recipients here.
rules.utcBadgeTitle: Universal time (UTC). Add your time zone offset for local time.
rules.triggerTransfer: When a task transfer is requested
rules.hourHelp: If you set an hour, we only evaluate in that UTC window. If you leave it empty, checks may run on each background pass (typically hourly). For local time, add your offset from UTC.
rules.clientFilterHint: If you pick clients, only their projects are considered.
rules.projectFilterHint: If you pick specific projects, the rule only evaluates those.
rules.evalCoherenceHelp: Coherence emails include a per-project month summary and, when relevant, a row per person with their share of the target and planned or logged hours.
rules.conditionsLabel: Notify if any of these apply
rules.thresholdHelp: We only notify if the project total or any employee is off by at least this many hours vs the deadline.
rules.deliveryPerProject: One email per project (subject shows status)
rules.deliveryDigest: One digest with several projects (max configurable)
rules.toastPreviewError: Could not generate the email preview.

coherence.in-rule: On track (only if you want OK alerts too)

recipientTransfer.all_with_hours_in_month: Everyone with hours on the project (month)

recipientScheduled.all_with_hours_in_month: Everyone with hours on the project (month)

previewDialog.title: Email preview
previewDialog.footerHint: Approximate view of what recipients will get. Gmail, Outlook, or others may show small format differences.

team.title: Role Management
team.description: Configure permissions and default access for each role in your agency.
team.permissionsSaveHint: Permission changes apply when you save agency settings.
team.organizationHint: Areas (name and color) are configured under Organization, the previous tab in this menu.
team.deleteDesc: This action cannot be undone and may affect assigned users.

projects.filtersDesc: Configure how projects are filtered in different views
projects.includeNote: Project name must contain AT LEAST ONE of these terms
projects.excludeNote: Project name must NOT contain ANY of these terms
projects.includeTooltip: Comma-separated terms. Project must contain AT LEAST ONE to appear.
projects.excludeTooltip: Comma-separated terms. Project must NOT contain ANY to appear.
projects.emptyNote: If you leave "Inclusion patterns" empty, all projects are included by default
projects.aliasingDesc: Configure rules to automatically rename projects (e.g., Kit Digital → KD:)
projects.aliasingInstruction: Define patterns to detect and rename special projects
projects.matchNote: If project contains any of these terms (case-insensitive)
projects.prefixNote: Text to be added at the beginning of the project name

integrations.title: Connections
integrations.description: Optional features (Weekly, CRM, demo) and ad accounts. The PPC module controls whether ad routes appear.
integrations.optionalFeaturesDesc: Enable or disable flows (Weekly, CRM, privacy in demo). These do not replace the PPC module: ad accounts are below.
integrations.adsPlatformsDesc: OAuth and Meta / Google Ads accounts. Requires PPC module on and role permissions to see ad screens.
integrations.weeklyCloseDayDesc: Last day of the operational week (from each week’s Monday). After that date, that week’s tasks can no longer be edited in the planner and must be handled through the Weekly close flow.
integrations.dependencyError: To activate "{{name}}", you must first activate: {{deps}}
integrations.crmUserIdWarning: If you disable "CRM User ID", "CRM Task Export" will also be disabled
integrations.crmPackDescription: Links your agency to your CRM or another system: each member can set their CRM user ID on their profile, and each project can have an external project ID. Users with permission can export the month’s tasks to CSV (task name, CRM user ID, external project ID, and hours).
integrations.crmPackLegacyHint: You only had the user ID field on profiles enabled. Turn the switch on to complete the integration (export and external project ID on project records).

weekly_feedback.name: Weekly
weekly_feedback.description: Stops direct planner edits once a week is closed; the team resolves carry-over through Weekly (log hours, postpone, transfer, split tasks, etc.).

crm_export.name: CRM Task Export
crm_export.description: Exports planning to CSV and enables a per-project external project ID (CRM) field so records match your external system.

crm_user_id.name: CRM User ID
crm_user_id.description: Adds a field on each team member’s profile for their ID in the CRM or another external system. Required for export.

anonymize_ads_for_video.name: Demo mode (hide sensitive data)
anonymize_ads_for_video.description: Throughout the application, visually replaces names of people, clients, projects, and tasks with generic labels.

weekly.title: Weekly close
weekly.description: Each person closes the week from the planner (Weekly button). The close day defines when a week is locked for direct edits.

googleAds.title: Google Ads
googleAds.description: Import investment and metrics from Google Ads campaigns.
googleAds.connectDesc: Google's consent screen will open. After authorizing, you can select your account.
googleAds.selectAccountDesc: Select the main or MCC account to sync.
googleAds.connectFirst: Connect with Google first to see your available accounts.
googleAds.configError: Configuration error: Agency customer ID missing.
googleAds.updateSuccess: Google Ads account updated. Sync again to load data.

metaAds.title: Meta Ads
metaAds.description: Import investment and metrics from Facebook and Instagram Ads.
metaAds.connectDesc: Meta login will open. After authorizing, available ad accounts will be imported.
metaAds.importDesc: Calls the Meta API and registers accounts in "Connected accounts".

dataExportHub.title: Data export for reports
dataExportHub.subtitle: JSON downloads per block and month. Contains personal and employment-related data—handle with care.
dataExportHub.scopeHint: Same as Profitability / Operations: empty department = whole agency.
dataExportHub.downloadZip: Download full ZIP (split by month & type)
dataExportHub.zipExplain: The ZIP includes employee schedule config, planning, deadlines, absences, events, feedback, profitability, radar, coherence, and a monthly burnout/capacity file. With more than 45 people and no department filter, per-employee JSON files are omitted (the aggregated burnout-by-employee.json remains).

clients.title: Clients
clients.subtitle: Manage your clients and their hour consumption
clients.emptySub: Try another search term or create your first client to start

agencies.title: My Agencies
agencies.hubIntro: Pick the active agency if you have more than one. The shortcuts below match Agency settings: profitability (hourly target €/h), computed vs actual hours, Connections (Weekly, CRM, ads), and menu modules.

guide.title: What you’ll find in each place
guide.lead: Summary aligned with the settings screen so Weekly, CRM, and the hourly target do not blur into each other.
guide.settingsLi1: General & profitability: agency name, slug, and effective hourly rate target (€/h) used on Financial health; positive values only, comma or dot as decimal, up to two decimal places.
guide.settingsLi2: Hours tracking: choose whether progress and profitability mainly use computed hours (what you bill or budget) or actual time spent; the timer is optional.
guide.settingsLi3: Connections — Weekly: the close day is when a week stops being editable directly in the planner; carry-over is handled in the Weekly flow. Meta and Google Ads accounts are set here when the PPC module is on.
guide.settingsLi4: Connections — CRM: one switch turns on profile CRM user ID, external project ID on each project, and monthly CSV export (task, IDs, and hours) together.
guide.settingsLi5: Organization, team tab (roles and menu permissions), projects (filters and aliasing), visible product areas (OKRs, Deadlines, timer…), appearance, and plan or billing.
guide.teamBody: People, departments, and day-to-day operational staff data. This is not the same screen as agency access administrators.
guide.adminsBody: Who has access to this agency in Taimbox: invite, remove, and transfer ownership when applicable. Internal roles (what each role sees in the menu) are configured under Team inside Agency settings.

agencies.description: Manage your agencies or create a new one.

departmentBanner.viewing: You are viewing the filtered view for:
departmentBanner.hidden: The rest of the data is hidden.

subscriptionBanner.exceeds: Your agency exceeds the limits of the Starter Plan. Upgrade to Pro or Business to plan again.

okrs.title: Objectives and Key Results (OKRs)
okrs.description: Manage and monitor strategic objectives and team performance.

dialog.projectDesc: Objective associated with an active project.

alert.title: Are you absolutely sure?
alert.description: This action cannot be undone. The objective will be permanently deleted.

operationsRadar.title: Operational Tracking
operationsRadar.description: Daily review of at-risk processes and deviations. Search and filters apply to the coherence report.
operationsRadar.noBlockingTasks: Nothing is blocking the team this month
operationsRadar.noBlockingDetails: All tasks that block others are completed or there are no dependencies this month.
operationsRadar.projectsDesc: How projects are doing this month. Filter by alert level if you need it.
operationsRadar.noFilteredProjects: No projects match the current filters.
operationsRadar.tooltipNoActivity: Projects with an objective but no planned or computed tasks this month
operationsRadar.tooltipNeedsPlanning: Projects with tasks pending completion this month
operationsRadar.tooltipBehindSchedule: Below objective or slow progress (low pace / low progress)
operationsRadar.tooltipInRule: Up to date, no excess and no tasks pending planning
operationsRadar.noResults: No results with the current filters.
operationsRadar.noLifecycleRisk: No deliverables with lifecycle risk right now.
operationsRadar.tooltipLifecycleRisk: Deliverables with consumption or projection risk across the phase (does not replace the monthly status).
operationsRadar.lifecycleSectionHint: Summary of the full lifecycle; it does not use the radar's month filter.
operationsRadar.actionLowPace: Low pace to reach the objective.
operationsRadar.coherenceTasksDialogSubtitle: This project's tasks for the month
operationsRadar.coherenceTasksDialogSubtitleDeliverable: Month in view: aligned with this month's deadline. In “Prior months” you see phase tasks from before this month.
operationsRadar.coherenceTasksPriorHint: Only tasks whose week falls in the deliverable phase and in months before {{month}}.
operationsRadar.coherenceTasksNoPrior: No tasks in prior months within this phase.
operationsRadar.coherencePhaseTotalsFootnote: “Operational”: closed per agency tracking preference; open = assigned estimates.
operationsRadar.coherenceNoDeadlineBanner: This project is not on this month's deadline; the row below sums the team's planned and computed hours.
operationsRadar.coherenceDeadlineDeltaTooltip: Not the same as “To compute”: (month Plan + Computed) minus the hours agreed in Deadlines. Negative: headroom under the agreement; positive: above the agreement.
operationsRadar.coherenceNoDeadlineSumTooltip: No deadline baseline this month: planned + computed team hours (flagged because there is nothing to compare in Deadlines).

tooltip.unknownCost: Cost underestimated: some tasks are assigned to deleted employees

deliverableLifecycle.costModeIndependent: Cost mode is independent from the monthly view. Default is standard.
deliverableLifecycle.bannerUnconfigured: {{count}} deliverables without phase dates. Set start/end to see consumption.
deliverableLifecycle.emptyDesc: Configure a Deliverable project with phase dates or adjust filters.

pitch.welcome: Welcome. Remember why your team needs this discipline.

members.title: Agency Members
members.description: Manage agency members and their roles
members.empty: There are no members in this agency

dialogs.deleteDesc: This will unlink {{name}} from this agency. If they are not in any other Taimbox agency, their data in this agency and their login account will also be deleted.
dialogs.lastAdminWarning: ⚠️ This is the last administrator. You cannot remove them.
dialogs.transferDesc: Select the new owner of the agency. The current owner will lose their owner privileges.
dialogs.warningDesc: This action will transfer the agency ownership to the selected member.

toast.noPermission: You do not have permission to access this page
toast.noAccess: You do not have access to this agency
toast.lastAdminError: You cannot remove the last administrator of the agency
toast.userDeleted: Member removed: data in this agency and login deleted (no other agencies)
toast.userUnlinked: User unlinked from this agency. Their login account still exists and they may have data in other agencies.

onboarding.footer: Everything you set here can be edited later in Agency settings (name, modules, departments, connections, etc.).

wizard.timeAndReadyFirst: Usually quick. If you leave, we save your place so you can continue later.

agencyProfile.title: How your agency works
agencyProfile.desc: Set your financial reference, how you count time, and which parts of Taimbox you want to use.

integrations.title: Connections to how you work
integrations.desc: Optional: Weekly integration (lock past weeks in the planner + close via Weekly), CRM, and more. You can leave everything off and configure it later under Connections.

departments.title: Departments
departments.desc: Teams or areas (SEO, design, accounts…). They filter the planner and assign ownership.

roles.title: Roles
roles.desc: Labels like “Account” or “Designer”. You can refine permissions later in agency management.

employees.title: Team
employees.desc: Invite by email (no password here): they get a link to sign in. You can skip this step.

client.title: First client
client.desc: The brand or company that pays you. Projects sit under a client.

project.title: First project
project.desc: A specific piece of work (campaign, retainer, website…). Set expected hours and monthly fee.

ehr.label: Target effective hourly rate (€/h)
calloutBullets: Target average revenue ÷ hours for profitability tracking (Financial health).
It is not what you bill on every project.
Positive values only; comma or dot as decimal; up to two decimal places.
ehr.whatIs: The hourly rate you aim to achieve on average (revenue ÷ hours) to cover cost and margin. Taimbox uses it in financial health views and alerts; it is not necessarily what you bill on each project.
ehr.placeholderHint: Review or adjust the value; if you leave it empty we will use the placeholder suggestion.

hours.label: How do you want Taimbox to treat hours?
hours.computedHelp: You can track time spent separately from what you bill or budget: e.g. 1.5 h worked and 2 h “computed” for complexity or margin. Choose this when that commercial adjustment is part of how you work.
computedBullets: Two figures: time spent vs. what you bill or budget.
E.g. 1.5 h worked and 2 h computed for margin or complexity.
Best when that commercial adjustment is routine.
hours.actualHelp: The main figure is time truly spent; computed hours stay the same as actual. You do not have to use the timer—manual entry is fine. Pick this if you do not need that commercial split.
actualBullets: Computed hours stay equal to actual.

modules.title: Product features
modules.sectionIntroShort: Each switch shows or hides a menu entry. You can change this later in Agency settings.
modules.sectionIntro: Each option turns on a section in the menu. If you turn something off, the team will not see it until you enable it again in Agency settings.
modules.deadlinesSummary: Tasks with due dates tracked by client or project (alongside the planner).
modules.deadlinesHelp: Task lists with due dates: see what is due, reorder, and track work by client or project. This does not replace the hour planner; it works alongside it.
modules.trackerSummary: Per-task timer to capture real time against the plan.
modules.trackerHelp: Each person can start and stop a timer per task to record real time. Useful if you bill or compare actual hours to the plan.
modules.okrsSummary: Measurable goals per person or team (OKR area in the menu).
modules.okrsHelp: Measurable objectives and key results by person or team (quarterly goals, milestones, etc.). This is the “OKRs” area in the menu; turn it off if you do not use that framework.
modules.ppcLead: Official Google Ads and Meta reporting and links are an advanced part of Taimbox, with API cost and technical setup.
modules.ppcBody: By design this is not part of initial signup: when you need it, you turn it on calmly under Agency settings → Connections, using the OAuth linking the platforms require.
modules.ppcReassurance: You do not need to decide now: the planner, clients, and the rest of the product work without PPC.
modules.ppcPlanNote: Your current plan does not include this module.

tracker.maxHint: Sessions longer than this stop automatically so forgotten timers do not run forever.

calloutBullets: Everything here is optional; the same toggles live under Agency settings → Connections.
Weekly: once a week is closed, the planner stops manual edits for that week.
CRM: one switch for IDs on profiles and projects plus monthly CSV export.
integrations.weeklyCardHelp: With Weekly on, closed weeks are not rewritten by hand in the calendar—changes go through the Weekly flow. Turn it off if you prefer full freedom.
integrations.weeklyCloseHelpShort: Last day of the operational week (from Monday). After that day, that week is locked for direct planner edits.
integrations.crmPackShort: One switch: CRM user ID on profiles, external project ID, and monthly CSV export (task, IDs, hours).
integrations.intro: You can skip this for now and turn features on later in Connections (agency settings) once you know your workflow.
integrations.weeklyExtra: Enables stricter history: the planner won’t let you rewrite weeks that are already closed; changes go through the Weekly close. Leave it off if you prefer to edit the past freely.
integrations.skipToast: You can configure this later in Connections whenever you want.
integrations.weeklyCloseHelp: End of the operational week (from that week’s Monday). After it, that week no longer allows direct planner edits—you use Weekly. It does not have to be Friday.
integrations.crmIntro: One switch: CRM user ID on profiles, external project ID on projects, and monthly CSV export with task, user ID, project ID, and hours.

calloutBullets: An area or team for planner filters and project ownership.
You need at least one to continue the wizard.
departments.addHintShort: Name and colour; then set each card’s default planner view.
departments.stepIntro: A department is a group of people or a business area. It is used for colour and filters in the planner and to show who “owns” a project.
departments.addHint: Create at least one to organise the planner and assign owners.
departments.mustCreate: Add at least one department to continue.

dept.viewWeeklyHelp: See workload by week on the calendar (team planning).
dept.viewDailyHelp: Focused on one day: tasks and blocks for the current day.
dept.strictHelp: When on, this department cannot freely switch planner view.

calloutBullets: A label per person (Account, Creative, etc.).
“Administrator” can manage the agency; other names are yours to define.
Menu permissions are refined later under Agency settings → Team.
roles.intro: Roles are labels you assign to each person (e.g. Account, Creative). “Administrator” can manage the agency; others are custom. Fine-grained permissions are adjusted later in team management.

calloutBullets: Optional step: invite now or later from Team.
Without “Add to list”, nothing is saved and no email is sent.
If you continue with people on the list, the wizard sends invitations.
team.noPasswordShort: Email only: each person sets their password from the invitation link.
team.hintShort: This step is optional. If you fill in details, use the main button below to add that person to the list; invitations are sent when you continue the wizard.
team.hint: Optional: add colleagues now or later. They receive an email with a link to create a password (you do not set a password here). Hours per day feed weekly capacity in the planner.
team.listLabel: People on the list (invited when you press Continue)
team.noPassword: There is no password field here—only email. Each person sets their password from the invitation link.
team.addCta: Add to list and prepare invitation
team.addHelper: If you do not press this button, nothing above is saved: no email is sent until they are on the list and you press Continue.
team.skipExplainer: “Skip” means you continue on your own for now. You can invite people later from Team. No email is sent in this step if the list is empty.
team.scheduleHelp: How many hours that person usually works each day (not a holiday calendar). Values are summed for weekly capacity.
team.duplicateEmail: That email is already on the list

calloutBullets: The brand or company that pays you.
Several projects can sit under one client.
Colour is visual only in tables and the planner.
client.intro: The client is the brand or company that pays you. You can have several projects under the same client (campaigns, months, deliverables…). Colour helps spot them in tables and the planner.
client.name: Client name
client.nameHelp: Trading or legal name as your team knows it.
client.colorHelp: Visual only: bars and tags wherever this client appears.

calloutBullets: A specific engagement (campaign, retainer, site…).
Monthly hours = planned load.
Monthly fee = what the client pays that month for this project (not an hourly rate).
project.intro: The project is the specific engagement (e.g. “SEO Q2” or “Website maintenance”). Monthly hours are the planned load; monthly fee is what the client pays for this project each month.
project.name: Project name
project.nameHelp: A name everyone understands; you can include client or month if it helps.
project.deptHelp: Which area leads the project in reports and filters. You can leave it unassigned if it does not apply yet.
project.typeHint: Internal project label; the list is shared for all agencies (custom types from settings may come later).
project.budgetHelp: Hours you plan to spend on this project per month on average. Used to compare plan vs actual in reports.
project.minHHelp: Monthly floor you guarantee the client; use 0 if not applicable.
project.monthlyFeeHint: Total amount the client pays per month for this project (not an hourly rate).

pacing.recommendedDailySub: to close the month on budget
pacing.googleNote: Taimbox calculates the monthly budget by summing the daily limits (x30.4) of your active campaigns in Google Ads. You can override this budget manually here to compare against a different target.
pacing.metaNote: The spending limit in Meta Ads is configured in Facebook (campaign, set or shared budget). Here you define the monthly target budget in Taimbox to compare real pace vs. target.
pacing.paceWarningGoogle: Spending pace is above the target to not exceed the monthly budget. Review campaigns in Google Ads, this usually happens if daily budgets sum more than the monthly target divided by 30.
pacing.paceWarningMeta: Spending pace is above the target to not exceed the monthly budget. Review and adjust limits in Meta Ads according to your structure (campaign, set or ad account).
pacing.forecastTooltip: Uses the average daily spend of the month (spent ÷ days elapsed) projected to the full calendar. If you recently changed the pace, it may not match the average daily target; that value is the pace to close within Taimbox's target budget.

configAccount.title: Configure account
configAccount.groupNote: Accounts from the same group are consolidated.
configAccount.hideAccountNote: Will not appear in the list

splitAccounts.title: Split Accounts
splitAccounts.description: Separate campaigns by keyword into virtual accounts.
